questions.question: Explain the difference between embedded documents and document references in MongoDB. When would you use each approach?
expectedPoints: Embedded documents store related data within a single document
Document references store related data in separate documents with references
Use embedded documents for data that is frequently accessed together
Use document references when data relationships are many-to-many or when data size is large

questions.question: Describe MongoDB's approach to horizontal scaling through sharding. Include the key components and how they work together to distribute data and process queries.
expectedPoints: Sharding distributes data across multiple machines (shards)
Config servers store metadata about chunk distribution and shard key ranges
Mongos routers act as query routers for client applications
Shard key determines how data is distributed across shards
Chunks are ranges of shard key values that are distributed evenly
Balancer moves chunks between shards to maintain balance
Queries are routed to appropriate shards based on shard key
